context_only: # 0.23.0 — Audit Redesign, Enforcement, Round Memory, and Domain Lenses
### 2.4 Prompt-level field naming (avoid confusion with finding category)
### 3.4 No spec excerpt for non-spec roles
### 4.4 No full parser bundled in v0.23.0
### 5.2 Task-to-file mapping and per-role selection rules

tasks.title: Add Task gate_skipped_reason field
tasks.acceptance:
- Task.GateSkippedReason string field with omitempty json tag exists
- tp reopen clears gate_skipped_reason
- tp set rejects gate_skipped_reason as managed field
tasks.closed_reason:
- Task.GateSkippedReason string field with omitempty json tag exists at internal/model/task.go:34, round-trip tested
- tp reopen clears gate_skipped_reason at internal/cli/reopen.go:59
- tp set rejects gate_skipped_reason as managed field at internal/cli/set.go:25, test TestSetRejectsGateSkippedReason green

tasks.title: Add Workflow gate timeout checks cap fields
tasks.acceptance:
- Workflow gains GateTimeoutSeconds default 600, Checks default empty, ReviewMaxRounds and AuditMaxRounds default 0 via UnmarshalJSON
- every row of the 6.7 field table serializes with stated defaults
tasks.closed_reason:
- Workflow gains GateTimeoutSeconds default 600, Checks default [], ReviewMaxRounds/AuditMaxRounds default 0 via UnmarshalJSON at internal/model/workflow.go:41; explicit zero preserved
- 6.7 field table serialization: TestWorkflow_FieldTableSerialization asserts gate_timeout_seconds:600, checks:[], review_max_rounds:0, audit_max_rounds:0 round-trip; go test ./... + golangci-lint run green

tasks.title: Enforce workflow field write and read rules
tasks.acceptance:
- tp set --workflow rejects out-of-range gate_timeout_seconds and max_rounds with exit 1
- hand-edited out-of-range values fall back to 600 and 0 at read time with tp validate warnings
- quality_gate stays read-only via tp set
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp set --workflow: gate_timeout_seconds 30-3600, review/audit_max_rounds outside the 0-50 range give output.Error + exit 1 (internal/cli/set.go workflowFieldRange)
- For manually corrupted values, the Workflow.Effective* helpers fall back to 600/0 (internal/model/workflow.go); tp validate produces a warning for the same cases (validateConvergence)
- quality_gate stays inside readOnlyWorkflowFields, tp set --workflow rejects it

tasks.title: Implement shared RunCommand runner
tasks.acceptance:
- RunCommand runs sh -c on Unix and cmd /C on Windows with inherited env and caller cwd
- 64 KB ring buffer captures combined output with parameterized tail size
- timeout counts as failure with timed-out message
tasks.closed_reason:
- RunCommand at internal/engine/runcmd.go:64 uses sh -c on Unix, cmd /C on Windows (runtime.GOOS), cmd.Env inherited (nil), cmd.Dir = caller-provided dir
- tailBuffer bounds combined stdout+stderr to 64 KB (runBufferSize), tail(n) parameterizes reported line count
- context.WithTimeout + DeadlineExceeded branch returns Passed=false, ExitCode=nil, Message 'timed out after <N>s'

tasks.title: Test RunCommand success failure timeout
tasks.acceptance: - TestRunCommand_Success_Failure_Timeout passes per the 19.1 description
tasks.closed_reason: - TestRunCommand_Success_Failure_Timeout in internal/engine/runcmd_test.go: exit 0 -> Passed, exit 3 -> failure + output tail, sleep 5 + 1s timeout -> TimedOut + 'timed out after 1s' message; the tail parameter and cwd/env inheritance are also in sub-tests; go test ./... green

tasks.title: Rewrite VerifyClosure with evidence-line rule
tasks.acceptance:
- ExtractKeywords stopWords technicalTermRegex and length check deleted per 7.1
- N>=2 criteria require N column-0 evidence lines; indented bullets do not count; N<=1 passes on non-empty reason
- forbidden patterns retained and covered-by skips verification; error hint enumerates criteria per 7.4
tasks.closed_reason:
- ExtractKeywords, stopWords, technicalTermRegex, filePathRegex and the min-length check were deleted; the gatePassed parameter was removed from the VerifyClosure signature (internal/engine/closure.go)
- For N>=2 criteria, N column-0 '- ' evidence lines are required (CountEvidenceLines); indented bullets are not counted; N<=1 passes with a non-empty reason
- Forbidden patterns were retained, coveredBy skips verification; ClosureError.Hint counts criteria numerically, the cli done/close call sites use ClosureHint

tasks.title: Test closure verification v2
tasks.acceptance: - TestVerifyClosure_EvidenceLines TestVerifyClosure_ForbiddenPatternsRetained TestVerifyClosure_KeywordMachineryGone pass per 19.1
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestVerifyClosure_EvidenceLines in internal/engine/closure_test.go: 3 criteria + 3 column-0 lines passes, 3+2 fails by naming the counts, indented lines are not counted, 1 criterion + free text passes
- TestVerifyClosure_ForbiddenPatternsRetained: the deferral pattern, a single word, and a covered-scope claim containing no path are rejected
- TestVerifyClosure_KeywordMachineryGone: Turkish prose criteria pass with a matching evidence-line count

tasks.title: Wire gate into done and close
tasks.acceptance:
- every row of the 6.1 trigger table implemented; gate runs once per invocation after cheap checks
- gate runs pre-flock then flock re-read re-validates state atomically for single and multi per 6.2 item 6
- gate_passed_at stamped on success per 6.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- 6.1 table: done single/multi/batch/auto-commit and close run the gate once per invocation (internal/cli/gate.go runQualityGatePreFlock, batchHasSurvivor); tp commit does not run the gate; --covered-by does not skip the gate; the cheap checks (checkDoneTarget) run before the gate
- The gate runs BEFORE the flock; then re-read + re-validate inside the flock is preserved with the existing bodies (done.go/close.go pre-flock blocks); on failure exit 4, no task closes (TestGate_FailureClosesNothingWithExit4)
- On success every closed task gets a gate_passed_at stamp (gateRan || doneGatePassed); 6 new TestGate_* tests + whole suite + lint green

tasks.title: Gate failure error output
tasks.acceptance:
- exit 4 JSON error carries gate_cmd exit_code output_tail and hint per 6.4
- timeout reports gate timed out after Ns with null exit_code
tasks.closed_reason:
- The exit 4 JSON error carries gate_cmd, exit_code, output_tail and hint (gate.go runQualityGatePreFlock/emitBatchGateFailure; TestGate_FailureClosesNothingWithExit4 verifies the fields)
- Timeout gives a 'gate timed out after Ns' message + null exit_code: gateFailureMessage and TestGateFailureMessage_TimeoutReportsSecondsAndNullExitCode (internal/cli/gate_unit_test.go)

tasks.title: Implement --skip-gate escape hatch
tasks.acceptance:
- all 8 items of the 6.5 list implemented including empty-reason exit 2, no gate execution, recorded reason, show and report display
- --skip-gate with --gate-passed is exit 2
tasks.closed_reason:
- The 8 items of 6.5: empty reason exit 2 (done+close), the gate is not run (TestSkipGate_RecordsReasonAndSkipsExecution), gate_skipped_reason is recorded + gate_passed_at null, recorded even in a project without a gate (TestSkipGate_RecordedEvenWithoutGate), show is automatic (omitempty) + a gate_skipped_reason field was added to report, reopen cleanup and tp set rejection exist from a previous task (reopen.go:59, gate_skip_field_test.go)
- Batch: per-entry skip_gate (pointer field), an empty/whitespace value drops the entry into failures and is not counted as a skip (TestSkipGate_BatchEmptySkipGateFailsEntry), if all are skipped the gate never runs (TestSkipGate_BatchAllSkipNeverRunsGate), even if the gate fails the skip entries close (TestSkipGate_BatchEntriesCloseDespiteGateFailure)
- --skip-gate + --gate-passed exit 2, --skip-gate + --batch exit 2 (TestSkipGate_UsageErrors); whole suite + lint green

tasks.title: Gate batch semantics
tasks.acceptance:
- cheap-check failures land in failures pre-gate; gate runs iff a surviving non-skip entry exists; skip entries close regardless of gate result per 6.1 and 6.4
- --skip-gate flag with --batch is exit 2; empty skip_gate entry fails into failures and does not count as skip
tasks.closed_reason:
- Ucuz-kontrol hatalari failures'a dusuyor ve gate'i tetiklemiyor (batchNeedsGate; TestGateBatch_CheapCheckFailuresDoNotTriggerGate); gate yalniz skip'siz survivor varsa 1 kez kosuyor (TestGateBatch_MixedCheapFailureAndSurvivorRunsGateOnce); skip entry'ler gate sonucundan bagimsiz kapaniyor (TestSkipGate_BatchEntriesCloseDespiteGateFailure)
- --skip-gate + --batch exit 2 (TestSkipGate_UsageErrors); bos skip_gate entry failures'a dusuyor ve skip sayilmiyor (TestSkipGate_BatchEmptySkipGateFailsEntry, batchNeedsGate cheap-check dali)

tasks.title: Keep --gate-passed compatibility
tasks.acceptance:
- flag ignored with info when quality_gate set; attestation preserved when unset per 6.6
- flag no longer affects closure verification
tasks.closed_reason:
- When quality_gate is set the flag is ignored + a 'quality gate is executed by tp; --gate-passed ignored' info (done single/multi pre-flock; TTY-only because output.Info is silent in JSON mode); the stamp comes from the real gate run (TestGatePassedCompat_IgnoredWithInfoWhenGateSet)
- When quality_gate is empty the attestation is preserved, gate_passed_at is stamped via the flag (TestGatePassedCompat_AttestationPreservedWithoutGate); the flag no longer affects closure verification (gatePassed was removed from the VerifyClosure signature in engine-closure-v2)

tasks.title: Test gate execution paths
tasks.acceptance: - TestDoneGate_RunsOncePerInvocation TestDoneGate_AllSkipBatchNoRun TestDoneGate_SkipGateRecorded TestDoneGate_GatePassedCompat pass per 19.2
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestDoneGate_RunsOncePerInvocation: a batch of 3 runs the gate once, a non-batch error closes no task, on a batch error only the skip entries close (internal/cli/donegate_test.go)
- TestDoneGate_AllSkipBatchNoRun, TestDoneGate_SkipGateRecorded (including set rejection + reopen cleanup), TestDoneGate_GatePassedCompat (flag ignoring + attestation + skip-gate combination exit 2) all pass; suite + lint green

tasks.title: Parse spec YAML frontmatter
tasks.acceptance:
- tp: mapping parsed via gopkg.in/yaml.v3 with domain and lens keys per 12.1
- unterminated block treated as content with lint error; malformed YAML stays excluded with defaults per 12.2 item 8
- non-string domain, non-mapping lens, unknown lens keys, non-list values, non-string elements produce lint warnings and are ignored
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp: mapping'i gopkg.in/yaml.v3 ile parse ediliyor; domain (default software) ve lens (implementer/tester/architect/all) anahtarlari okunuyor, diger top-level anahtarlar yok sayiliyor (internal/engine/frontmatter.go; go.mod'a ilk YAML bagimliligi eklendi)
- Kapanmayan blok icerik sayiliyor + lint error; kapali-ama-bozuk YAML bloklarda blok excluded kaliyor, defaults uygulaniyor + parse hatasi lint error (TestParseFrontmatter_StructuralFailures)
- Non-string domain, non-mapping lens/tp, bilinmeyen lens anahtari, non-list deger, non-string eleman: hepsi lint warning + ignore (TestParseFrontmatter_ShapeWarnings, _NonMappingLensAndTP)

tasks.title: Exclude frontmatter from all parsers
tasks.acceptance: - all 8 exclusion surfaces of the 12.2 list implemented with absolute line numbers preserved
tasks.closed_reason:
- BlankFrontmatter/BlankFrontmatterLines (engine/frontmatter_blank.go) preserves absolute line numbers by replacing the block with blank lines; 8 surfaces are wired: countContentLines, ParseHeadings, cli lint parseSpecFile (lint rules + structured extraction use the same lines), DiffSections calls (review.go + review_verify.go), readSpecContent (inline embed), audit.go specData, ExtractSpecExcerpt (plan/show/next spec_excerpt) skip the fm lines
- The structural-corruption behavior comes from the parser: an unclosed block is Present=false so its content is counted, in malformed YAML the block stays excluded; TestBlankFrontmatter_ParserExclusion verifies 3 surfaces by absolute line number

tasks.title: Report frontmatter in lint output
tasks.acceptance: - tp lint --json emits frontmatter object with present lines domain lens_roles per 12.4 including the exact no-frontmatter shape
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp lint --json frontmatter nesnesi {present, lines ('1-K'), domain, lens_roles} emit ediyor; lens_roles bos-olmayan listeli anahtarlar implementer,tester,architect,all sirasiyla (lint.go lintFrontmatter; TestLintFrontmatter_ObjectShapes)
- Frontmatter'siz spec'te tam sekil {present:false, lines:null, domain:'software', lens_roles:[]}; bozuk YAML parse hatasini adlandiran lint error + defaults (ayni testin alt vakalari)

tasks.title: Test frontmatter parse and exclusion
tasks.acceptance: - TestFrontmatter_ParseAndExclusion TestFrontmatter_MalformedYAML TestFrontmatter_Unterminated pass per 19.1
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestFrontmatter_ParseAndExclusion: tp mapping parse + content lines, headings, structured elements, DiffSections and spec_excerpt are cleared of the block; absolute line numbers are preserved (internal/engine/frontmatter_named_test.go)
- TestFrontmatter_MalformedYAML: a closed-but-malformed block stays excluded, defaults + lint error; TestFrontmatter_Unterminated: an opened-but-unclosed block counts as content + lint error, the heading inside it is parsed

tasks.title: Implement audit category enum
tasks.acceptance:
- 5 constants with IsValidCategory; ResolveAuditCategory enforces the 2.2 precedence
- RenderAuditCategoryText renders enum plus precedence from shared constants per 4.4
- category presence rules of 2.3 documented in rendered schema text
tasks.closed_reason:
- 5 constants (security, concurrency, error-handling, correctness, contract) + IsValidCategory; ResolveAuditCategory applies the 2.2 precedence via the auditCategoryPrecedence slice (internal/engine/audit_category.go)
- RenderAuditCategoryText produces the enum + precedence text from the SAME constant slice (4.4 single-source requirement)
- The 2.3 presence rules (category on every line; PASS -> explicit null; PARTIAL/FAIL -> enum, not null) are documented in the rendered text

tasks.title: Implement audit file filtering
tasks.acceptance:
- task-to-file mapping via commit_sha with alphabetical list ordering per 5.2; drop rules filter universe first per 5.5
- per-role selection ranking caps and fallback implemented for all three roles; --affected-files replaces the universe
- affected_files exposed as path tasks diff_summary per 5.6
tasks.closed_reason:
- Task-to-file mapping via commit_sha (GitTaskFileMapping: git show --name-only, intersection with the universe, a task with no sha/unknown sha gets zero files), per-file task ids and lists alphabetical; the drop rules filter the universe FIRST (binary, testdata/**, *.golden, deleted ones) - caps are filled with the next eligible files (internal/engine/auditfiles.go SelectAuditFiles/filterAuditUniverse)
- The three roles' selection: spec-coverage task-count desc + alphabetical tie-break cap 20 and an empty-union first-20 fallback; security uses a path OR HEAD first-200-line substring heuristic (a file not in HEAD only via path), alphabetical cap 20; maintainability first 10; when --affected-files changes the universe the same rules apply (input-injection design), files outside the diff +0/-0
- affected_files as {path, tasks, diff_summary} in AuditFileEntry; the named TestFilterFiles_* tests come in the engine-audit-tests task

tasks.title: Test audit categories and filtering
tasks.acceptance:
- TestAuditCategory_EnumValid TestAuditCategory_PrecedenceResolution TestRenderAuditCategoryText_ContainsEnumAndPrecedence pass
- TestFilterFiles_SpecCoverage_RanksByTaskCount Cap20 Security Maintainability DropsBinaryFixturesDeleted DropFirstBackfill pass per 19.1
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestAuditCategory_EnumValid, _PrecedenceResolution, TestRenderAuditCategoryText_ContainsEnumAndPrecedence: the 5-value enum, precedence resolution, and the rendered text's enum+precedence+presence content (internal/engine/auditfiles_test.go)
- TestFilterFiles_SpecCoverage_RanksByTaskCount (task count desc, alphabetical tie, +0/-0 fallback), _Cap20 (spec-coverage and security), _Security (path + HEAD-content heuristic, a file not in HEAD only via path, alphabetical), _Maintainability (alphabetical first 10), _DropsBinaryFixturesDeleted (including the fallback list), _DropFirstBackfill (13-3=10, the tail is filled from raw 11-13) all pass

tasks.title: Rework auditPrompt output schema
tasks.acceptance:
- auditPrompt drops Category and gains ChecklistItems and AffectedFiles fields; ChecklistItem Type enum includes finding per 3.2
- NDJSON output schema of section 4 embedded as prompt guidance including the class field row
tasks.closed_reason:
- auditPrompt'ta Category alani yok (testte anahtar yoklugu dogrulaniyor); ChecklistItems ([]ChecklistItem, 3.2 Type enum'u finding dahil yorumda belgeli) ve AffectedFiles ({path, tasks, diff_summary}) alanlari eklendi ve dolduruluyor (audit.go, audit_schema.go; TestAuditSchema_PromptFieldsAndOutputSchema)
- 4. bolum NDJSON semasi renderAuditOutputSchema ile her prompt'a gomuluyor: alan tablosu class satiri dahil, evidence_lines cift formu, kategori enum + oncelik metni RenderAuditCategoryText'ten (tek kaynak); eski 'PASS|PARTIAL|FAIL' cikti-format satirini asserte eden test yeni semaya guncellendi

tasks.title: Route checklist items to roles
tasks.acceptance:
- spec items and finding items route to spec-coverage; file-level checklists to security and maintainability per 1.2
- empty roles omitted and prompts emitted in fixed order per 5.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- 1.2 disjoint routing: table_row/list_item/task_acceptance and finding items go to spec-coverage; security and maintainability carry file-based synthetic file_check items (file-sec-<n>/file-maint-<n>, in role-list order), no item appears in two prompts (routeChecklist, internal/cli/audit_roles.go; TestAuditRouting_DisjointAndFixedOrder)
- 5.3: an empty role is skipped from the prompts (the security no-match case is in the test), the prompts are in the fixed spec-coverage -> security -> maintainability-conventions order; one prompt per role (the old 50-item split was removed, old tests were adapted to the new behavior)

tasks.title: Rewrite generateAuditPrompts
tasks.acceptance:
- body order and per-item JSON embedding per 3.1 and 3.2 with deterministic ids including file-sec-n file-maint-n finding-n and pinned checklist order
- expected_evidence per 3.3 never empty; CLAUDE.md excerpt rule and lookup per 3.4
- Output Schema block embeds category enum and precedence text
tasks.closed_reason:
- 3.1 body order (Role -> Role Rules -> Spec Excerpt only for spec-coverage -> Project Context only for maintainability -> JSON-array Checklist -> Affected Files (max 20) in the 5.4 format -> Output Schema) in buildRolePrompt; the ordering is in the test via index comparisons (TestAuditPrompts_BodyOrderAndEmbedding)
- 3.2 deterministic ids: table-t-r/list-l-n/task-<id> existing schemas, file-sec-<n>/file-maint-<n> by role-list index, finding-<n> by file index; pinned ordering (table+list spec_line ascending, then task, then finding); regeneration from the same input is byte-equal (TestAuditPrompts_DeterministicRegeneration)
- 3.3 expected_evidence is never empty (task->files mapping, section-search fallback, inspect-file, verify-the-fix-120-char); 3.4 the CLAUDE.md excerpt is found in task-file dir -> git root order, the '## Conventions' span with a 50-line cap, cut off at the next heading (claudeMDExcerptFor; the 'ignored' section is excluded in the test)

tasks.title: Test audit prompt generation
tasks.acceptance: - TestRouteChecklist_Disjoint TestGenerateAuditPrompts_ThreeRolesPresent EmptyRoleOmitted StructuredItems NoCategoryField SpecExcerptOnlyForSpecCoverage CLAUDEmdOnlyForMaintainability pass per 19.2
tasks.closed_reason: - Seven named tests pass per the 19.2 specifications: TestRouteChecklist_Disjoint and TestGenerateAuditPrompts_EmptyRoleOmitted (rename + spec comment, audit_routing_test.go), TestGenerateAuditPrompts_ThreeRolesPresent / _StructuredItems (JSON-array body = ChecklistItems) / _NoCategoryField / _SpecExcerptOnlyForSpecCoverage / _CLAUDEmdOnlyForMaintainability (audit_named_test.go, with the shared three-role setup)

tasks.title: Implement review state engine
tasks.acceptance:
- state directory layout and initial state.json written by first creator per 8.1 and 8.2
- flock-guarded writes, round arithmetic, consecutive_clean, spec_hash staleness computed per 8.2 and 8.6
- corrupt or index-less directory aborts with exit 3; missing round file skipped with warning
tasks.closed_reason:
- 8.1 directory layout (ReviewStateDir: <spec-dir>/.tp-review/<spec-base>) and the initial {spec, review_rounds:[], audit_rounds:[]} index are written by the first creator (EnsureReviewState, internal/engine/reviewstate.go)
- Writes are under the state.json flock (WithReviewStateLock, over the existing WithFileLock); round arithmetic is len+1, ConsecutiveClean tail-counting, SpecHash sha256:<hex> and StateStale/Converged per 8.6 (TestConsecutiveCleanAndStaleness)
- A corrupt state.json or an index-less-but-non-empty directory aborts with StateCorruptError (exit 3 + a 'repair or delete <path>' hint to be wired in the cli); a missing round file can be skipped and warned about via LoadRoundRows found=false, it keeps being counted in the round arithmetic

tasks.title: Test review state engine
tasks.acceptance: - TestReviewState_RoundLifecycle TestReviewState_Staleness TestReviewState_CorruptIndexAborts pass per 19.1
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestReviewState_RoundLifecycle: kayit degistirilemez entry ekliyor (sonradan --resolve round dosyasini degistirse de clean/findings donmus kaliyor), clean-dirty-clean-clean -> consecutive_clean 2 (internal/cli/review_state_named_test.go)
- TestReviewState_Staleness: son kayitli turdan sonra spec duzenlenince stale=true + unconverge; eslesen hash converged tutuyor
- TestReviewState_CorruptIndexAborts: bozuk state.json VE indekssiz round dosyasi vakalari review/record/status/import genelinde exit 3 + 'repair or delete' hint'i; state.json byte'lari degismeden kaliyor (sessiz rebuild yok); engine-seviyesi primitifler reviewstate_test.go'da zaten testli

tasks.title: Implement workflow resolution chain
tasks.acceptance:
- discovery chain then spec-adjacent then defaults resolution with absolute path comparison per 8.6
- all six workflow fields resolved through the single chain
tasks.closed_reason:
- ResolveWorkflow (internal/engine/workflow_resolve.go): discovery chain -> spec-adjacent <base>.tasks.json -> DefaultWorkflow; the spec field is absolutized against the task-file dir, the CLI argument against CWD, then compared (specMatches)
- Six workflow fields return from a single chain (tf.Workflow as a whole; defaults 2/2 clean, 0 cap, 600s, empty checks); 3 branches tested with TestResolveWorkflow_*

tasks.title: Integrate state into review prompt generation
tasks.acceptance:
- auto round numbering, snapshot write, auto previous-findings into role prompts only per 8.3 items 1-3
- flag interaction matrix of 8.3 item 6 including --round conflict, --no-state behavior and review_loop shape, mode exclusivity
- state participation by perspective per 8.3 closing paragraph
tasks.closed_reason:
- 8.3 items 1-3: R = recorded round + 1 is auto-numbered, a snapshot-round-<R>.md byte copy is written (on regeneration before recording the same file is overwritten), previous findings are injected into the THREE role prompts from the round-1..R-1 files with identity-key dedup, the --findings source overrides (TestReviewStateRounds_Lifecycle)
- 8.3 item 6 matrix: a --round conflicting with the state-derived R gives exit 2 + a hint pointing to --no-state; --no-state turns off all reads/writes, echoes the round flag, adds a no-record note to the convergence text; --no-state + --record/--status/--check exit 2 (TestReviewStateRounds_RoundConflictAndNoState); the old manual-flow tests were moved to --no-state
- Perspective participation: documentation/testing/code-audit do not touch the state (the block after the perspective returns; TestReviewStateRounds_PerspectivesDoNotTouchState)

tasks.title: Inject snapshot diff into prompts
tasks.acceptance:
- newest-earlier-snapshot diff block with 40-line and 6000-char caps per 8.3 item 4
- missing snapshot omits block with info; --diff-from overrides and forces at any round
tasks.closed_reason:
- At R>=2 the newest round<R snapshot is taken as baseline and a 'Changed sections since round <k>' block is added to EVERY prompt via DiffSections; the per-section 40-line and total 6000-character caps are in buildChangedSectionsBlock (internal/cli/review_autodiff.go; TestReviewAutoDiff_SnapshotBaseline, _SectionLineCap)
- If there is no earlier snapshot the block is skipped and an info line is printed; --diff-from overrides the baseline and forces the block in every round (including R=1) - the old 'round >= 2' restriction was removed (TestReviewAutoDiff_DiffFromForcesAtRound1)

tasks.title: Emit review_loop schema v2
tasks.acceptance:
- every row of the 8.7 table implemented; max_rounds removed; convergence text is no findings surviving verification
- instruction names record and status --check loop
tasks.closed_reason:
- All rows of the 8.7 table: round is state-derived (a conflicting flag exit 2, from a previous task), max_rounds was completely removed from the struct (all modes; TestReviewBasic verifies its absence), required_clean_rounds/consecutive_clean/converged/stale are filled from the state via pointer fields, omitted under --no-state
- the convergence text is 'no findings surviving verification (any severity) in <N> consecutive rounds'; the instruction names the sub-agent -> merge -> --record -> repeat loop up to '--status --check exit 0' (TestReviewRound1WithFindings updated assertions)

tasks.title: Implement tp review --record
tasks.acceptance:
- parse rules with blank-line skip and wontfix duplicate fixed row handling per 8.4 items 1-2 including empty-evidence rejection
- round file copy plus immutable index append with frozen clean flag; mechanize_candidates across all recorded rounds in output
tasks.closed_reason:
- 8.4 line rules: skip empty line, invalid JSON aborts exit 1 with a line number, pre-resolved fixed aborts, an empty-evidence wontfix exit 1, wontfix does not dirty the round, duplicate dirties it; zero lines is a clean round (parseRecordRows; TestReviewRecord_RowRules + _Lifecycle)
- The file is copied as review-round-<R>.ndjson (round file first, then index; under the state flock), the clean flag is frozen at record time; the output computes mechanize_candidates over ALL recorded rounds + register hint (TestReviewRecord_MechanizeCandidatesInOutput); corrupt state exit 3 + repair hint (TestReviewRecord_CorruptStateAborts)

tasks.title: Implement tp review --status --check
tasks.acceptance:
- status shape with budget_exhausted presence rule and full empty-state shape per 8.5
- --check runs checks and exits per converged and stale; bare --check exit 2
tasks.closed_reason:
- --status prints the 8.5 shape: review_rounds, consecutive_clean, required_clean_rounds (via the 8.6 chain), converged, stale, mechanical_checks (the recorded list without run results); the full shape + exit 0 on empty state (TestReviewStatus_EmptyStateFullShape); budget_exhausted only when cap>0 (the 8.8 formula)
- --check runs all workflow.checks in the 6.2 environment, pass/fail + output_tail only on failures; exit 0 only when converged AND all checks pass (TestReviewStatus_CheckRunsChecksAndGatesExit); bare --check / --record --check / --status+mode combinations exit 2 (TestReviewStatus_FlagRules); runMechanicalChecks is the shared helper for 15.3

tasks.title: Implement tp audit --record --status
tasks.acceptance:
- non-PASS row counting, independent audit round sequence, spec_hash staleness per 10.1
- audit status shape without mechanical_checks plus empty-state shape and flag rejections per 10.2
- scope stays row counting aside from budget refusal per 10.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- 10.1: lines with a non-PASS/missing status field are counted as findings (countAuditFindings), the file is copied as audit-round-<N>.ndjson and appended to state.json.audit_rounds (a sequence independent of review; TestAuditRecord_CountsNonPass), spec_hash/staleness same as 8.6
- 10.2: the --status shape without mechanical_checks, empty-state shape exit 0, --check 0 only when converged (TestAuditStatus_Shapes); --record/--status are mutually exclusive and --affected-files/--findings rejection exit 2 (TestAuditRecordStatus_FlagRejections)
- 10.3: --record only counts lines, does not read class, no mechanize_candidates (the absence of the output key is verified in the test)

tasks.title: Test record and status machinery
tasks.acceptance: - TestReviewRecord_CleanAndDirty TestReviewStatus_Check TestAuditRecord_CountsNonPass TestAuditStatus_Check TestAuditRecordStatus_FlagRejections pass per 19.1 and 19.2
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestReviewRecord_CleanAndDirty: an empty file is clean, whitespace skipping, a parse error exit 1 with a line number, all-wontfix clean, pre-resolved fixed exit 1, empty-evidence wontfix exit 1, duplicate dirties it (internal/cli/record_status_named_test.go)
- TestReviewStatus_Check: converged+fresh+passing checks 0, if any one of the three conditions fails 1, plain --status lists the checks without running them (with file-write proof); TestAuditStatus_Check: converged 0 with audit_clean_rounds, dirty/stale 1, no mechanical_checks in the shape; TestAuditRecord_CountsNonPass and TestAuditRecordStatus_FlagRejections exist from a previous task (internal/cli/audit_record_test.go)

tasks.title: Implement round budgets
tasks.acceptance:
- generation refusal with escalation hint for review and audit per 8.8; budget_exhausted formula and status-only placement
- --record beyond-cap refusal evaluated before parsing and writes for review and audit
- import enforcement never relaxed by the cap
tasks.closed_reason:
- The review default mode and audit prompt generation refuse with exit 4 and an escalation hint (uncounted pass -> user-approved cap increase / --force) when the cap is full + unconverged (refuseIfBudgetExhausted, budget.go; TestRoundBudget_ReviewRefusals/_AuditRefusals); the cap-triggered state read inherits the corrupt abort
- budget_exhausted = cap>0 AND rounds>=cap AND NOT converged; only in --status outputs and when cap>0 (settled in previous tasks, verified in tests); a converged run is not refused even at the cap (TestRoundBudget_ConvergedAtCapNotRefused)
- The --record refusal is BEFORE line parsing and any kind of state write (the round file not being created is verified in the test); import enforcement is not affected by the cap (the enforcement 9.1 task never reads the cap)

tasks.title: Test round budget escalation
tasks.acceptance: - TestRoundBudget_Escalates passes per 19.2 with review and audit fixtures
tasks.closed_reason: - TestRoundBudget_Escalates covers the entire 19.2 spec: both caps 2, two dirty rounds each; review prompt generation exit 4 + escalation hint, beyond-cap review/audit --record exit 4, both --status shapes budget_exhausted:true, audit generation blocked the same way, import enforcement exit 1 independent of the cap (internal/cli/budget_escalates_test.go); suite + lint green

tasks.title: Enforce convergence at import
tasks.acceptance:
- state lookup with pinned path bases, consecutive_clean and staleness checks, divergence warning per 9.1
- failure hints including the exhausted-budget escalation sequence; --force bypass; zero-task shell imports without --force
- no-state info line per 9.2
tasks.closed_reason:
- The state directory is found by a pinned path (target task-file dir + spec basename); if there is a recorded round, consecutive_clean>=required and a staleness check, a failing import exit 1 (TestImportEnforcement_Convergence unconverged/stale sub-tests); when the governing value diverges from the 8.6-resolved value, a warning naming both values
- The error hint names tp review --record + --force; when the budget is full the hint turns into the 8.8 escalation series (exhausted_budget sub-test); --force skips both checks; a zero-task init shell can be overwritten without --force, a file with real tasks requires --force (TestImport_ShellOverwriteNoForce)
- When there is no state / no round, import passes with a 'review convergence not verified (no recorded rounds)' info (no_state sub-test); it never relaxes cap enforcement

tasks.title: Preserve workflow on import
tasks.acceptance: - raw workflow-key detection carries existing workflow for wrapped and bare-array imports per 9.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- The top-level workflow-key probe in the raw JSON (importedHasWorkflowKey, internal/cli/import_workflow.go) runs BEFORE the struct defaults; if the target file exists the existing workflow block is carried over - for both the wrapped and bare-array paths (TestImport_WorkflowPreservation 4 sub-tests)
- Bare-array wrapping now gets the DefaultWorkflow + status='open' default (the root of the v0.22.0 live bug: zero workflow + empty status failed validation); an import carrying a workflow key writes its own workflow

tasks.title: Test import enforcement
tasks.acceptance: - TestImport_ConvergenceEnforced TestImport_WorkflowPreserved TestImport_ShellOverwriteNoForce pass per 19.2
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestImport_ConvergenceEnforced: unconverged exit 1, stale exit 1, --force bypass, only an info when there is no state (import_enforce_test.go - the name was aligned to 19.2)
- TestImport_WorkflowPreserved: a workflow-key-less --force re-import preserves quality_gate AND checks (the checks assertion was added); TestImport_ShellOverwriteNoForce: a zero-task shell can be overwritten without --force, enforcement stays armed, a file with tasks requires --force

tasks.title: Implement regression perspective
tasks.acceptance:
- standalone modes a and b with vacuous-input usage error and read-only state per 11.1
- prompt body order per 11.3 with 50-entry fixed-findings cap
- category enum gains regression per 11.4
tasks.closed_reason:
- Mode (a) stateful (R>=2, read-only: snapshot/state.json don't change - the directory listing is equal in the test) and mode (b) --diff-from+--findings (never touches the state); missing/partial input exit 2, an empty diff + zero fixed findings also exit 2 + a hint reflecting the 8.3-item-5 condition (internal/cli/review_regression.go; TestRegression_* three tests)
- 11.3 body order: persona, changed-sections block (same construction as 8.3-4), 50-capped newest-first fixed findings ('finding - evidence' lines), three numbered checks, the standard finding format; per 15.3 the mechanical checks also run in regression mode and add an exclusion block
- 11.4: the category enum gained the regression value (findingFormat); since merge/resolve/report are map-based they accept it

tasks.title: Auto-include regression prompt
tasks.acceptance:
- 4th prompt appended under the 8.3 item 5 condition with process-first instruction per 11.2
- delta-pass usage documented in instruction text
tasks.closed_reason:
- On the condition R>=2 AND (diff block non-empty OR >=1 fixed finding), regression is added as the 4th prompt (review.go regressionIncluded block; collectFixedFindings shared); when the condition is not met 3 prompts remain (TestRegressionAutoInclude three sub-tests - including the only-fixed-finding branch)
- the instruction says 'Process the regression prompt first...' and documents the 11.2 uncounted delta-pass usage; the auto-diff test was updated to the 4-prompt expectation

tasks.title: Test regression perspective
tasks.acceptance: - TestRegressionPrompt_AutoInclusion passes per 19.2
tasks.closed_reason: - TestRegressionPrompt_AutoInclusion covers the 19.2 spec with five sub-tests: inclusion when only a diff or a fixed finding exists (two trigger branches + a non-inclusion branch), standalone input is required exit 2, verifying that standalone does not change the state.json bytes or the directory contents (internal/cli/regression_autoinclude_test.go)

tasks.title: Implement domain personas and lens injection
tasks.acceptance:
- every row of the 12.3 table implemented: persona switch and three dropped software questions for non-software domains
- lens.all and per-role questions appended in pinned order to role and regression prompts
tasks.closed_reason:
- 12.3 tablosunun tum satirlari: non-software domain'de uc persona degisiyor (execute-exactly / verify-every-claim / internal-consistency), implementer'in happy-path sorusu ve architect'in backward-compat + performance sorulari dusuyor; yapisal ve tester'in vague/divergent sorulari domain-notr kaliyor (promptDomain, TestDomainLens_NonSoftwarePersonasAndDrops + _SoftwareDomainUnchanged)
- Lens: lens.all rol ve regression prompt'larinin numarali listesine, lens.<role> yalniz kendi roluna, sira hardcoded -> all -> role-specific (appendLensQuestions; regression yalniz all aliyor — TestDomainLens_RegressionGetsAllLens; siralama index karsilastirmasiyla dogrulandi)

tasks.title: Test lens injection
tasks.acceptance: - TestLensInjection_DomainSwitch passes per 19.2
tasks.closed_reason: - TestLensInjection_DomainSwitch exactly per the 19.2 spec: verifies the prose domain persona changes, the three dropped software questions, and the hardcoded->all->role-specific lens order (internal/cli/domain_lens_test.go; the helper tests _SoftwareDomainUnchanged and _RegressionGetsAllLens are in the same file)

tasks.title: Implement section-derived coverage
tasks.acceptance:
- span rule per 13.1 and covered-set union per 13.2 with warning only on empty union
- anchor requirement error and per-task unresolvable-section warnings per 13.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- 13.1 span rule in sectionSpans (internal/engine/sectionspan.go): from L to the next heading of level <=V or to end of file; sub-headings included
- 13.2 union: ValidateLineCoverage unites source_lines + the resolved source_sections spans; the 'no coverage computable' warning only when the union is empty (linecoverage.go)
- 13.3: if one of the two anchors is missing, a schema error (validateSchema); ambiguous/unresolved entries a warning by the section-anchor rule, an error under --strict (validate.go); two cli tests were adapted to the new behavior

tasks.title: Test section-derived coverage
tasks.acceptance: - TestSectionSpan_Derivation TestLineCoverage_SectionDerived pass per 19.1
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestSectionSpan_Derivation: the span ends before the next same-or-higher-level heading, the last section runs to EOF, sub-sections included (internal/engine/sectionspan_test.go)
- TestLineCoverage_SectionDerived: a task with only source_sections covers its span; the union with explicit source_lines was verified (the gap in between is reported)

tasks.title: Add finding class field
tasks.acceptance:
- reviewFinding gains Class with omitempty; merge and resolve preserve it; first non-empty wins on dedup per 14.1
- finding format instruction documents the optional class slug
tasks.closed_reason:
- reviewFinding.Class was added with an omitempty tag (review.go:22); because resolve is map-based it preserves the class (TestFindingClass_ResolvePreservesClass); in merge dedup the first non-empty class wins, the identity key did not change (review_merge.go dedupEntry.class; TestFindingClass_MergePreservesAndFirstNonEmptyWins)
- the findingFormat instruction documents the optional kebab-case class slug with the 14.1 text (script-checkable pattern, otherwise omit)

tasks.title: Report class grouping and candidates
tasks.acceptance:
- by_class breakdown in report per 14.2
- mechanize_candidates rule with populations, tie-break and register hint per 14.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- The by_class breakdown (lines with a non-empty class; the first non-empty class per identity-key) was added to the JSON and TTY output (computeClassBreakdown, review_report.go)
- mechanize_candidates: the >=2 distinct rounds OR >=5 in a single round rule, the population is the argument NDJSONs for --report, total desc + alphabetical tie-break ordering (computeMechanizeCandidates); the register hint constant (mechanizeRegisterHint) is printed in the TTY and will be reused by the --record side

tasks.title: Test class reporting
tasks.acceptance: - TestReport_ByClassAndCandidates passes per 19.2
tasks.closed_reason: - TestReport_ByClassAndCandidates: verifies class grouping (excluding empty class), the >=2-round and >=5-single-round candidate rules, total desc ordering (internal/cli/report_class_test.go); the alphabetical tie-break is in a separate test; suite + lint green

tasks.title: Implement tp set --workflow checks
tasks.acceptance: - JSON array replace semantics with anchored slug uniqueness and non-empty cmd validation naming offending index per 15.1 and 15.2
tasks.closed_reason:
- checks='<json-array>' is written with full-list replacement semantics (set.go checks branch; TestSetWorkflowChecks_ReplaceAndValidate replace sub-test)
- engine.ValidateChecks: anchored ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$ slug, uniqueness in the array, non-empty cmd; on violation exit 1 and the offending index is named (checks[N] format, 3 cases in the test)

tasks.title: Execute mechanical checks at review
tasks.acceptance:
- checks run in default and regression modes, workflow-derived even under --no-state, with resolved timeout and cwd per 15.3
- mechanical_checks output with failure tails; invalid entries skipped with info and validate warning
- prompt exclusion block and instruction per 15.5; --status --check integration per 15.4
tasks.closed_reason:
- The checks run in order before prompt generation in the default review mode (8.6-resolved workflow, resolved gate_timeout, cwd = task-file dir; runMechanicalChecks shared helper); because they are workflow-derived they also run under --no-state and emit an exclusion (TestChecksExecution_RunsUnderNoState); when the regression perspective arrives it will use the same helper
- The mechanical_checks output field {class, cmd, passed, exit_code} + output_tail only on failures (last 20 lines); failures do not stop generation (TestChecksExecution_PromptExclusion); an invalid entry is skipped with an info during exec, tp validate gives a warning (ValidateChecks was added to validateConvergence)
- The 15.5 exclusion block on every prompt with the class list, the instruction adds 'fix those failures before spawning sub-agents'; the 15.4 --status --check integration is in a previous task (TestReviewStatus_Check)

tasks.title: Test checks validation and execution
tasks.acceptance: - TestWorkflowChecks_Validation TestChecksExecution_PromptExclusion TestValidate_GateTimeoutRange pass per 19.1 and 19.2
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestWorkflowChecks_Validation: an invalid slug, duplicate class, empty cmd are each rejected with the index name (internal/engine/checks_named_test.go)
- TestValidate_GateTimeoutRange: the value 9999 produces a validate warning, EffectiveGateTimeoutSeconds falls back to 600; TestChecksExecution_PromptExclusion exists from a previous task (internal/cli/checks_execution_test.go)

tasks.title: Write integration tests
tasks.acceptance:
- TestAudit_FullShape TestAudit_FileFilterCap TestAudit_NoLegacyCategoryField pass per 19.3
- TestSelfLoop_ReviewToImport and TestGateLoop_EndToEnd pass per 19.3
tasks.closed_reason:
- TestAudit_FullShape: end-to-end over a small spec, 3 role-labeled prompts in the JSON + the required prompt/checklist_items/affected_files fields on every prompt, expected_evidence never empty on the items (internal/cli/integration_test.go)
- TestAudit_FileFilterCap: 50 files -> security<=20, maintainability<=10; TestAudit_NoLegacyCategoryField: no category key in the audit JSON
- TestSelfLoop_ReviewToImport: lint -> init -> review -> dirty round -> import exit 1 -> 2 clean rounds -> import success -> spec edit -> import stale exit 1 (the importcmd ordering fix works via b5d8404); TestGateLoop_EndToEnd: gate fail blocks, gate pass gate_passed_at stamp, --skip-gate reason record

tasks.title: Rewrite SKILL.md per section 16
tasks.acceptance: - all 12 numbered items of section 16 reflected in SKILL.md including workflow order, review recipe, closure rules, gate and budget escalation policy, migration section, tail protocol
tasks.closed_reason:
- The 12 items of section 16 were incorporated into SKILL.md (skills/tp/SKILL.md): Workflow A order (interview -> lint -> init --quality-gate -> set --workflow budgets/checks -> review -> decompose -> plain import), review loop recipe (merge -> record -> resolve -> regression delta pass in >3 sections -> --status --check exit 0), closure rule 5 was deleted + evidence-line format, gate automatic + --skip-gate/budget escalation user approval, --gate-passed was dropped in Workflow B + automatic gate note, audit loop --record/--status --check
- Migration to v0.23.0 audit section (the 17 schema breakdown), frontmatter domain/lens guide, class/checks guide + mechanize candidate, decomposition rule 4 (source_sections required, source_lines optional), .tp-review/ state directory guide (commit to VC, prunable, CI implication), tail protocol (--verify wontfix + class-sweep) - all added

- backward compatibility tables of section 17 reflected in migration documentation
tasks.closed_reason:
- REFERENCE.md documents the new flags/fields/formats: --skip-gate, the full workflow-fields table (gate_timeout_seconds/review-audit_max_rounds/checks), review --record/--status/--check/--perspective regression/--no-state, audit --record/--status/--check, the .tp-review/ state directory schema, tp: frontmatter, finding class + report, NDJSON skip_gate; the 17 schema breakdown table is in the Audit JSON Schema section
- README.md command tables + Closure Verification (evidence-line format) + automatic gate section + audit 3-role/NDJSON + round-memory review flow were updated; CLAUDE.md self-dev flow (init --quality-gate -> set --workflow -> review loop -> plain import), the source_sections required rule, the automatic gate + user-approval escalation rule, workflow fields + gate_skipped_reason managed, .tp-review VC rule; already-implemented old ideas were cleaned from the list
